candidate_problems.text: What precise principle or mechanism determines the map from microscopic quantum vacuum structure to the gravitating large-scale stress-energy, and how can that map suppress naive EFT vacuum contributions while still preserving the observed rigidity of a nearly constant dark-energy component?

Which empirical signatures would unavoidably follow if the QFT-to-GR coupling is emergent or constrained rather than naive—for example in structure formation, gravitational response to phase transitions, equivalence-principle tests, or departures from w = -1—and can these signatures cleanly distinguish this program from mere fine-tuning or added-field models?

How can a theory make the decoupling of vacuum energy from curvature genuinely load-bearing rather than a rephrased cancellation—i.e., what consistency conditions, symmetries, boundary principles, or global constraints force the suppression and remain stable under radiative corrections, EFT matching, and changes of cutoff or renormalization scheme?
